title.undefined: Classification of medical devices and in vitro diagnostic devices according to the European MDR and IVDR regulations

description.undefined:
Description: An ontology designed to map the various high-risk medical devices, as well as in vitro diagnostic devices, in accordance with European regulations under the MDR (medical devices in classes I, IIa, IIb, III) and IVDR (in vitro diagnostic devices in classes A, B, C, D), linking devices to their manufacturers, risks, reported incidents, and corrective actions.
We do this using the LOT methodology.
Purpose and use case: to standardize and enable interoperability of information from various sources, facilitating market surveillance and, above all, regulatory compliance. With this ontology, we perform logical inferences to automatically identify risk levels or dependencies between incidents and safety actions. It can be useful for a health authority that wants to consult the safety history of a specific device, check risks, the corrective actions taken, and other information about the device.

title.value: Classification of medical devices and in vitro diagnostic devices according to the European MDR and IVDR regulations

Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency

MDR or IVDR, depending on the type of device
Relation between the device and its risk class (important)
The date when corrective action began
The date when corretive actions end
Relation between a Manufacturer and the corresponding competent authority
It can take the next values: high, medium or low
True if the medical is implantable, false if it is not.
Relation between incident and a risk
Relation between a device and its incident
Relation between the corrective action and its incident
True if the medical is active, false if it is not.

Level of severity of a risk. 
Values: critical, serious, moderate.
Relation between the incident and the medical device
Relation between incident and its manufacturer
True if the medical is ivd, false if it is not.
The date when incident was detected
True if the medical is reusable, false if it is not.
Relation between risk and its category
Type of affected device in a specific risk
